commands.name: add
commands.description: Add content to your library from a provider
commands.longDescription:
Discovers content from a provider and adds it to your library (~/.syllago/content/).

Without a positional argument, shows what content is available (discovery mode).
Provide a type or type/name to add content.

Syllago handles format conversion automatically. Once added, content can be
installed to any supported provider with "syllago install --to <provider>".

After adding, use "syllago install" to activate content in a provider.

Hooks-specific flags (--exclude, --scope) are only meaningful when adding hooks.

flags.name: --all
flags.description: Add all discovered content (cannot combine with positional target)

flags.name: --base-dir
flags.description: Override base directory for content discovery

flags.name: --dry-run
flags.description: Show what would be written without actually writing

flags.name: --exclude
flags.description: Skip hooks by auto-derived name (hooks only)

flags.name: --force
flags.description: Overwrite existing item without prompting

flags.name: --from
flags.description: Provider to add from (required)

flags.name: --name
flags.description: Display name for hooks/MCP (stored in .syllago.yaml metadata)

flags.name: --no-input
flags.description: Disable interactive prompts, use defaults

flags.name: --scope
flags.description: Settings scope to read from: global, project, or all (hooks/mcp only)

flags.name: --source-registry
flags.description: Registry name for taint tracking

flags.name: --source-visibility
flags.description: Source registry visibility (public, private, unknown)

commands.examples:
# Discover available content (read-only)
  syllago add --from claude-code

  # Add all rules from a provider
  syllago add rules --from claude-code

  # Add a specific rule by name
  syllago add rules/security --from claude-code

  # Add everything
  syllago add --all --from claude-code

  # Preview what would be written
  syllago add --from claude-code --dry-run

commands.name: capmon
commands.description: Capability monitor pipeline
commands.longDescription: Fetch, extract, diff, and report on AI provider capability drift.

commands.name: capmon diff
commands.description: Show field-level changes in provider-capabilities since a git ref

flags.name: --provider
flags.description: Limit diff to this provider slug

flags.name: --since
flags.description: Git ref to diff against (default: HEAD~1)

commands.name: capmon extract
commands.description: Run extraction on cached sources

flags.name: --provider
flags.description: Extract only this provider slug

commands.name: capmon fetch
commands.description: Fetch source URLs and update hash cache

flags.name: --provider
flags.description: Fetch only this provider slug

commands.name: capmon generate
commands.description: Regenerate per-content-type views and spec tables from provider-capabilities YAML

commands.name: capmon run
commands.description: Run the full capability monitor pipeline

flags.name: --dry-run
flags.description: Skip Stage 4 PR/issue creation; write report to stdout

flags.name: --provider
flags.description: Limit to this provider slug

flags.name: --stage
flags.description: Pipeline stage to run: 'fetch-extract' or 'report' (default: all stages)

commands.name: capmon seed
commands.description: Bootstrap or re-seed provider capability YAML from extracted data

flags.name: --cache-root
flags.description: Path to .capmon-cache/ (default: .capmon-cache)

flags.name: --force-overwrite-exclusive
flags.description: Allow overwriting provider_exclusive entries (prints warning)

flags.name: --provider
flags.description: Seed only this provider slug

commands.name: capmon test-fixtures
commands.description: Report fixture staleness or update fixtures for a provider

flags.name: --provider
flags.description: Provider slug for --update (required with --update)

flags.name: --update
flags.description: Re-fetch live source and update fixture files

commands.name: capmon verify
commands.description: Validate provider-capabilities YAML against JSON Schema

flags.name: --cache-root
flags.description: Path to .capmon-cache/ (default: .capmon-cache)

flags.name: --migration-window
flags.description: Accept current-minus-one schema_version during schema migrations

flags.name: --staleness-check
flags.description: Check last-run.json age and open issue if stale

flags.name: --threshold-hours
flags.description: Hours before a run is considered stale (used with --staleness-check)

commands.name: compat
commands.description: Show provider compatibility matrix for a content item
commands.longDescription:
Analyzes a library item and shows which providers support it,
what warnings arise during conversion, and which providers cannot
handle the content type at all.

For each provider, syllago attempts the full canonicalize-then-render
pipeline and reports the result.
commands.examples:
# Show compatibility for a skill
  syllago compat my-skill

  # JSON output for scripting
  syllago compat my-skill --json

commands.name: completion
commands.description: Generate the autocompletion script for the specified shell
commands.longDescription:
Generate the autocompletion script for syllago for the specified shell.
See each sub-command's help for details on how to use the generated script.

commands.name: completion bash
commands.description: Generate the autocompletion script for bash
commands.longDescription:
Generate the autocompletion script for the bash shell.

This script depends on the 'bash-completion' package.
If it is not installed already, you can install it via your OS's package manager.

To load completions in your current shell session:

	source <(syllago completion bash)

To load completions for every new session, execute once:

#### Linux:

	syllago completion bash > /etc/bash_completion.d/syllago

#### macOS:

	syllago completion bash > $(brew --prefix)/etc/bash_completion.d/syllago

You will need to start a new shell for this setup to take effect.

flags.name: --no-descriptions
flags.description: disable completion descriptions

commands.name: completion fish
commands.description: Generate the autocompletion script for fish
commands.longDescription:
Generate the autocompletion script for the fish shell.

To load completions in your current shell session:

	syllago completion fish | source

To load completions for every new session, execute once:

	syllago completion fish > ~/.config/fish/completions/syllago.fish

You will need to start a new shell for this setup to take effect.

flags.name: --no-descriptions
flags.description: disable completion descriptions

commands.name: completion powershell
commands.description: Generate the autocompletion script for powershell
commands.longDescription:
Generate the autocompletion script for powershell.

To load completions in your current shell session:

	syllago completion powershell | Out-String | Invoke-Expression

To load completions for every new session, add the output of the above command
to your powershell profile.

flags.name: --no-descriptions
flags.description: disable completion descriptions

commands.name: completion zsh
commands.description: Generate the autocompletion script for zsh
commands.longDescription:
Generate the autocompletion script for the zsh shell.

If shell completion is not already enabled in your environment you will need
to enable it.  You can execute the following once:

	echo "autoload -U compinit; compinit" >> ~/.zshrc

To load completions in your current shell session:

	source <(syllago completion zsh)

To load completions for every new session, execute once:

#### Linux:

	syllago completion zsh > "${fpath[1]}/_syllago"

#### macOS:

	syllago completion zsh > $(brew --prefix)/share/zsh/site-functions/_syllago

You will need to start a new shell for this setup to take effect.

flags.name: --no-descriptions
flags.description: disable completion descriptions

commands.name: config
commands.description: View and edit syllago configuration
commands.longDescription: Manage provider selection and preferences in .syllago/config.json.
commands.examples:
syllago config list
  syllago config add cursor
  syllago config remove windsurf

commands.name: config add
commands.description: Add a provider to the configuration
commands.examples: syllago config add cursor

commands.name: config list
commands.description: List current configuration
commands.examples:
# Show configured providers
  syllago config list

  # JSON output
  syllago config list --json

commands.name: config paths
commands.description: Manage custom provider path overrides
commands.longDescription:
Configure custom base directories or per-type path overrides for providers.
Overrides are stored in the global config (~/.syllago/config.json) since paths are machine-specific.
commands.examples:
syllago config paths show
  syllago config paths set claude-code --base-dir ~/custom/claude
  syllago config paths clear claude-code

commands.name: config paths clear
commands.description: Clear path overrides for a provider

flags.name: --type
flags.description: specific content type to clear (omit to clear all)

commands.examples:
# Clear all overrides for a provider
  syllago config paths clear claude-code

  # Clear only a specific type override
  syllago config paths clear cursor --type rules

commands.name: config paths set
commands.description: Set path overrides for a provider
commands.longDescription:
Set a base directory or per-type path override for a provider.
Paths must be absolute (start with / or ~/).

flags.name: --base-dir
flags.description: base directory override (replaces home dir)

flags.name: --path
flags.description: absolute path for the content type

flags.name: --type
flags.description: content type (e.g., skills, hooks)

commands.examples:
# Set a base directory for a provider
  syllago config paths set claude-code --base-dir ~/custom/claude

  # Set a per-type path override
  syllago config paths set cursor --type rules --path ~/my-rules

commands.name: config paths show
commands.description: Show configured path overrides

flags.name: --provider
flags.description: filter by provider slug

commands.examples:
# Show all path overrides
  syllago config paths show

  # Show overrides for a specific provider
  syllago config paths show --provider claude-code

commands.name: config remove
commands.description: Remove a provider from the configuration
commands.examples: syllago config remove windsurf

commands.name: convert
commands.description: Convert content between provider formats
commands.longDescription:
Transform content between provider formats using syllago's hub-and-spoke
conversion model (source -> canonical -> target).

Accepts either a file path or a library item name:
  - File path: reads the file directly (requires --from and --to)
  - Library name: looks up the item in your library (--from is optional)

Output goes to stdout by default, or to a file with --output.

flags.name: --diff
flags.description: Show unified diff between source and converted output

flags.name: --from
flags.description: Source provider (required for file input, optional for library items)

flags.name: --output
flags.description: Write output to this file path (default: stdout)

flags.name: --to
flags.description: Target provider (required)

flags.name: --type
flags.description: Content type for file input (rules, hooks, skills, agents, commands, mcp)

commands.examples:
# Convert a Cursor rule to Claude Code format
  syllago convert ./my-rule.mdc --from cursor --to claude-code

  # Convert a library item to Windsurf format
  syllago convert my-rule --to windsurf

  # Convert and save to a file
  syllago convert my-rule --to cursor --output ./cursor-rule.mdc

  # Convert a Copilot instructions file to Cursor
  syllago convert ./.github/copilot-instructions.md --from copilot-cli --to cursor --type rules

commands.name: create
commands.description: Scaffold a new content item in the global library
commands.longDescription:
Creates a new content item directory under ~/.syllago/content/ with
.syllago.yaml metadata.

flags.name: --provider
flags.description: Provider slug (required for rules, hooks, commands)

commands.examples:
# Create a new skill
  syllago create skills my-new-skill

  # Create a provider-specific rule
  syllago create rules my-rule --provider claude-code

  # Create a new agent
  syllago create agents my-agent

commands.name: doctor
commands.description: Check your syllago setup for problems
commands.longDescription:
Validates your syllago installation: library, config, providers,
installed content integrity, and registry configuration.

Each check reports [ok], [warn], or [err]. Exit code is 0 if all checks
pass, 1 if there are warnings, 2 if there are errors.
commands.examples:
syllago doctor
  syllago doctor --json

commands.name: explain
commands.description: Show detailed documentation for an error code
commands.longDescription:
Display documentation for a syllago error code.

Each error code has a detailed explanation including what it means,
common causes, and how to fix it.

flags.name: --list
flags.description: list all available error codes

commands.examples:
syllago explain CATALOG_001
  syllago explain --list

commands.name: info
commands.description: Show syllago capabilities
commands.longDescription: Machine-readable capability manifest. Useful for agents discovering syllago's features.
commands.examples:
# Show capabilities summary
  syllago info

  # JSON output for agent consumption
  syllago info --json

commands.name: info formats
commands.description: List supported file formats
commands.examples: syllago info formats

commands.name: info providers
commands.description: List all known providers
commands.examples:
# List providers and their supported types
  syllago info providers

  # JSON output
  syllago info providers --json

commands.name: init
commands.description: Initialize syllago for this project
commands.longDescription: Detects AI coding tools in use, creates .syllago/config.json with provider selection.

flags.name: --force
flags.description: Overwrite existing config

flags.name: --yes
flags.description: Skip interactive confirmation

commands.examples:
# Interactive setup
  syllago init

  # Skip confirmation prompts
  syllago init --yes

  # Overwrite existing config
  syllago init --force

commands.name: inspect
commands.description: Show details about a content item
commands.longDescription:
Display full details about a content item for pre-install auditing.

By default, shows metadata and the primary content file. Use --as to preview
what the content would look like converted to a specific provider's format.

Path formats:
  skills/my-skill                   Universal content (type/name)
  rules/claude-code/my-rule         Provider-specific (type/provider/name)

flags.name: --as
flags.description: Preview content converted to a provider's format

flags.name: --compatibility
flags.description: Show per-provider compatibility matrix (hooks only)

flags.name: --files
flags.description: Show file contents

flags.name: --risk
flags.description: Show detailed risk analysis

commands.examples:
# Inspect a skill (shows metadata + content)
  syllago inspect skills/my-skill

  # Preview what a rule looks like in Cursor format
  syllago inspect rules/coding-standards --as cursor

  # Compare formats side by side
  syllago inspect rules/coding-standards --as claude-code
  syllago inspect rules/coding-standards --as cursor

  # JSON output for scripting
  syllago inspect --json skills/my-skill

commands.name: install
commands.description: Activate library content in a provider
commands.longDescription:
Install content from your library into a provider's location.

By default uses a symlink so edits in your library are reflected immediately.
Use --method copy to place a standalone copy instead.

flags.name: --all
flags.description: Install all library content (cannot combine with a positional name)

flags.name: --base-dir
flags.description: Override base directory for content installation

flags.name: --dry-run
flags.description: Show what would happen without making changes

flags.name: --method
flags.description: Install method: symlink (default) or copy

flags.name: --no-input
flags.description: Disable interactive prompts, use defaults

flags.name: --to
flags.description: Provider to install into

flags.name: --to-all
flags.description: Install to all detected providers

flags.name: --type
flags.description: Filter to a specific content type

commands.examples:
# Install a skill to Claude Code
  syllago install my-skill --to claude-code

  # Install with a standalone copy instead of symlink
  syllago install my-skill --to cursor --method copy

  # Install all skills to a provider
  syllago install --to claude-code --type skills

  # Install to every detected provider at once
  syllago install --type skills --to-all

  # Install everything from your library
  syllago install --all --to claude-code

  # Preview what would happen
  syllago install my-skill --to claude-code --dry-run

commands.name: list
commands.description: List content items in the library
commands.longDescription:
Show a quick inventory of all content without launching the TUI.

By default, lists all content grouped by type. Use flags to filter.

flags.name: --source
flags.description: Filter by source: library, shared, registry, builtin, all

flags.name: --type
flags.description: Filter to one content type (e.g., skills, rules)

commands.examples:
# List all content grouped by type
  syllago list

  # Show only library items
  syllago list --source library

  # Show only skills
  syllago list --type skills

  # JSON output for scripting
  syllago list --json

commands.name: loadout
commands.description: Apply, create, and manage loadouts
commands.longDescription:
Loadouts bundle a curated set of syllago content — rules, hooks, skills,
agents, MCP servers — into a single shareable configuration.

Use "syllago loadout apply" to try or apply a loadout.
Use "syllago loadout create" to build a new loadout interactively.
Use "syllago loadout remove" to revert an active loadout.
commands.examples:
syllago loadout list
  syllago loadout apply my-loadout --try
  syllago loadout remove

commands.name: loadout apply
commands.description: Apply a loadout
commands.longDescription:
Apply a loadout to configure the current project for Claude Code.

Default mode (no flags): preview what would happen without making changes.
--try: apply temporarily; reverts automatically when the session ends.
--keep: apply permanently; run "syllago loadout remove" to undo.

flags.name: --base-dir
flags.description: Override base directory for content installation

flags.name: --keep
flags.description: Apply permanently

flags.name: --method
flags.description: Install method: symlink (default) or copy

flags.name: --preview
flags.description: Dry run: show planned actions without applying

flags.name: --to
flags.description: Target provider (overrides manifest provider; defaults to claude-code if unset)

flags.name: --try
flags.description: Apply temporarily; auto-revert on session end

commands.examples:
# Preview what a loadout would do
  syllago loadout apply my-loadout

  # Try temporarily (auto-reverts on session end)
  syllago loadout apply my-loadout --try

  # Apply permanently
  syllago loadout apply my-loadout --keep

  # Apply to a specific provider
  syllago loadout apply my-loadout --keep --to cursor

commands.name: loadout create
commands.description: Interactively create a new loadout
commands.examples: syllago loadout create

commands.name: loadout list
commands.description: List available loadouts
commands.examples:
# List all available loadouts
  syllago loadout list

  # JSON output
  syllago loadout list --json

commands.name: loadout remove
commands.description: Remove the active loadout and restore original configuration

flags.name: --auto
flags.description: Skip confirmation (used by SessionEnd hook)

commands.examples: syllago loadout remove

commands.name: loadout status
commands.description: Show active loadout status
commands.examples:
# Check if a loadout is active
  syllago loadout status

  # JSON output
  syllago loadout status --json

commands.name: manifest
commands.description: Manage registry manifests

commands.name: manifest generate
commands.description: Generate a registry.yaml for this repository
commands.longDescription:
Analyze the current repository and generate a registry.yaml manifest.
Run with --force to overwrite an existing registry.yaml. When overwriting,
a diff of added/removed/changed items is displayed before writing.

flags.name: --debug-skips
flags.description: show per-file skip reasons for content-signal classification

flags.name: --force
flags.description: overwrite existing registry.yaml

flags.name: --no-config
flags.description: suppress .syllago.yaml loading (use with --strict for fully explicit CI control)

flags.name: --scan-as
flags.description: scan path as content type: type:path (e.g. skills:Packs/)

flags.name: --strict
flags.description: disable content-signal fallback; fatal on missing scan-as paths

commands.name: registry
commands.description: Manage git-based content registries
commands.longDescription:
Add, remove, list, and sync git repositories as content registries.

Registries are read-only git repos containing shared content (skills, rules,
hooks, MCP configs, etc.). Use "registry sync" to pull updates, and
"registry items" to browse what's available.

To use registry content, browse it in the TUI ("syllago") or install it
directly with "syllago install --to <provider>".
commands.examples:
syllago registry add https://github.com/team/rules.git
  syllago registry sync
  syllago registry items --type skills

commands.name: registry add
commands.description: Add a git registry

flags.name: --name
flags.description: Override the registry name (default: derived from URL)

flags.name: --ref
flags.description: Branch, tag, or commit to checkout (default: repo default branch)

commands.examples:
# Add a registry by URL
  syllago registry add https://github.com/team/rules.git

  # Add with a custom name
  syllago registry add https://github.com/team/rules.git --name team-rules

  # Pin to a specific branch
  syllago registry add https://github.com/team/rules.git --ref main

commands.name: registry create
commands.description: Create a new registry
commands.longDescription:
Create a new registry in one of two modes:

  --new <name>          Scaffold an empty registry directory structure
  --from-native         Index provider-native content in the current repo

flags.name: --description
flags.description: Short description of the registry (used with --new)

flags.name: --from-native
flags.description: Index provider-native content in the current repo

flags.name: --new
flags.description: Scaffold an empty registry directory with this name

flags.name: --no-git
flags.description: Skip git init and initial commit (used with --new)

commands.examples:
# Scaffold an empty registry
  syllago registry create --new my-rules

  # Scaffold with a description
  syllago registry create --new my-rules --description "Team coding standards"

  # Index existing provider-native content
  syllago registry create --from-native

commands.name: registry items
commands.description: Browse content available in registries
commands.longDescription:
Lists content items from one or all registries.

Use --type to filter by content type. To install registry content, use
"syllago install --to <provider>" or browse in the TUI with "syllago".

flags.name: --type
flags.description: Filter by content type (skills, rules, hooks, etc.)

commands.examples:
# List all items from all registries
  syllago registry items

  # List items from a specific registry
  syllago registry items my-rules

  # Filter by content type
  syllago registry items --type skills

commands.name: registry list
commands.description: List registered registries
commands.examples:
# List all configured registries
  syllago registry list

  # JSON output
  syllago registry list --json

commands.name: registry remove
commands.description: Remove a registry and delete its local clone
commands.examples: syllago registry remove team-rules

commands.name: registry sync
commands.description: Pull latest content from one or all registries
commands.longDescription:
Runs git pull on registry clones to fetch the latest content.

Sync updates the local clone only — it does not modify your library or
installed provider content. Use "syllago registry items" to see what changed,
and "syllago install" to activate updated content.
commands.examples:
# Sync all registries
  syllago registry sync

  # Sync a specific registry
  syllago registry sync my-rules

commands.name: remove
commands.description: Remove content from your library and uninstall from providers
commands.longDescription:
Removes a content item from your library (~/.syllago/content/) and
uninstalls it from any providers where it is currently installed.

flags.name: --dry-run
flags.description: Show what would happen without making changes

flags.name: --force
flags.description: Skip confirmation prompt

flags.name: --no-input
flags.description: Disable interactive prompts, use defaults

flags.name: --type
flags.description: Disambiguate when name exists in multiple types

commands.examples:
# Remove a skill from library and all providers
  syllago remove my-skill

  # Disambiguate by type when name exists in multiple types
  syllago remove my-rule --type rules

  # Skip confirmation prompt
  syllago remove my-skill --force

  # Preview what would happen
  syllago remove my-skill --dry-run

commands.name: rename
commands.description: Set a human-readable display name for a content item
commands.longDescription:
Updates the display name stored in the item's .syllago.yaml metadata.
This is especially useful for hooks and MCP servers where the directory
name alone isn't descriptive enough.

The display name is shown in the TUI, list output, and anywhere items
are presented to users. The directory name (used for install paths and
references) is not changed.

flags.name: --name
flags.description: The new display name (required)

flags.name: --type
flags.description: Content type to disambiguate (skills, hooks, mcp, etc.)

commands.examples:
# Name a hook
  syllago rename my-hook --name "Pre-commit Linter"

  # Name an MCP server
  syllago rename postgres --name "Local Postgres Dev" --type mcp

  # Disambiguate by type when name exists in multiple types
  syllago rename my-item --name "Better Name" --type hooks

commands.name: sandbox
commands.description: Run and manage AI CLI tools in bubblewrap sandboxes
commands.longDescription:
Sandbox wraps AI CLI tools in bubblewrap to restrict filesystem access,
network egress, and environment variables.

Linux only: requires bubblewrap >= 0.4.0 and socat >= 1.7.0.
commands.examples:
syllago sandbox run claude-code
  syllago sandbox check
  syllago sandbox allow-domain api.example.com

commands.name: sandbox allow-domain
commands.description: Add a domain to the sandbox allowlist
commands.examples: syllago sandbox allow-domain api.example.com

commands.name: sandbox allow-env
commands.description: Add an env var to the sandbox allowlist
commands.examples: syllago sandbox allow-env OPENAI_API_KEY

commands.name: sandbox allow-port
commands.description: Add a localhost port to the sandbox allowlist
commands.examples: syllago sandbox allow-port 8080

commands.name: sandbox check
commands.description: Verify bubblewrap, socat, and optionally a provider
commands.examples:
# Check sandbox prerequisites
  syllago sandbox check

  # Check prerequisites for a specific provider
  syllago sandbox check claude-code

commands.name: sandbox deny-domain
commands.description: Remove a domain from the sandbox allowlist
commands.examples: syllago sandbox deny-domain api.example.com

commands.name: sandbox deny-env
commands.description: Remove an env var from the sandbox allowlist
commands.examples: syllago sandbox deny-env OPENAI_API_KEY

commands.name: sandbox deny-port
commands.description: Remove a localhost port from the sandbox allowlist
commands.examples: syllago sandbox deny-port 8080

commands.name: sandbox domains
commands.description: List allowed domains
commands.examples: syllago sandbox domains

commands.name: sandbox env
commands.description: List allowed env vars
commands.examples: syllago sandbox env

commands.name: sandbox info
commands.description: Show effective sandbox configuration
commands.examples:
# Show sandbox config
  syllago sandbox info

  # Show config with provider mount profile
  syllago sandbox info claude-code

commands.name: sandbox ports
commands.description: List allowed localhost ports
commands.examples: syllago sandbox ports

commands.name: sandbox run
commands.description: Run a provider in a sandbox

flags.name: --allow-domain
flags.description: Allow an additional domain for this session

flags.name: --allow-env
flags.description: Forward an additional env var into the sandbox

flags.name: --allow-port
flags.description: Allow a localhost port inside the sandbox

flags.name: --force-dir
flags.description: Skip directory safety checks

flags.name: --mount-ro
flags.description: Mount additional path read-only inside sandbox

flags.name: --no-network
flags.description: Block all network egress (no proxy)

commands.examples:
# Run Claude Code in a sandbox
  syllago sandbox run claude-code

  # Run with additional domain access
  syllago sandbox run claude-code --allow-domain api.example.com

  # Run with no network at all
  syllago sandbox run gemini-cli --no-network

commands.name: share
commands.description: Contribute library content to a team repo or registry
commands.longDescription:
Copies a library item to a target repo, stages the change, and
optionally creates a branch and PR.

By default, shares to the current team repo (the syllago repo you're in).
Use --to to share to a named registry instead.

flags.name: --no-input
flags.description: Skip interactive git prompts, stage only

flags.name: --to
flags.description: Target registry name (omit for current team repo)

flags.name: --type
flags.description: Disambiguate when name exists in multiple types

commands.examples:
# Share a skill to the current team repo
  syllago share my-skill

  # Share to a named registry
  syllago share my-skill --to my-registry

  # Disambiguate by type
  syllago share my-rule --type rules

  # Non-interactive mode (stage only, no git prompts)
  syllago share my-skill --no-input

commands.name: sync-and-export
commands.description: Sync registries then export content to a provider
commands.longDescription:
Convenience command that syncs all registries then installs.

Equivalent to running:
  syllago registry sync && syllago install --to <provider>

This is useful in CI/CD or automation where you want a single command
to ensure registries are up-to-date before installing.

flags.name: --dry-run
flags.description: Show what would be exported without making changes

flags.name: --llm-hooks
flags.description: How to handle LLM-evaluated hooks: skip (drop with warning) or generate (create wrapper scripts)

flags.name: --name
flags.description: Filter by item name (substring match)

flags.name: --source
flags.description: Which items to export: local (default), shared, registry, builtin, all

flags.name: --to
flags.description: Provider slug to export to, or "all" for every provider (required)

flags.name: --type
flags.description: Filter to a specific content type (e.g., skills, rules)

commands.examples:
# Sync registries and export to Cursor
  syllago sync-and-export --to cursor

  # Export to all providers
  syllago sync-and-export --to all --type skills

  # Export only registry content to Kiro
  syllago sync-and-export --to kiro --source registry

commands.name: telemetry
commands.description: Manage usage analytics settings
commands.longDescription: View and control anonymous usage data collection. Run 'syllago telemetry status' for details.
commands.examples:
syllago telemetry status
  syllago telemetry off
  syllago telemetry on
  syllago telemetry reset

commands.name: telemetry off
commands.description: Disable telemetry
commands.examples: syllago telemetry off

commands.name: telemetry on
commands.description: Enable telemetry
commands.examples: syllago telemetry on

commands.name: telemetry reset
commands.description: Generate a new anonymous ID
commands.longDescription:
Generates a new anonymous ID. Previously collected data under your old ID
is not deleted from PostHog. To request deletion, email privacy@syllago.dev
with your old ID.
commands.examples: syllago telemetry reset

commands.name: telemetry status
commands.description: Show telemetry state and anonymous ID
commands.examples: syllago telemetry status

commands.name: uninstall
commands.description: Deactivate content from a provider
commands.longDescription:
Removes installed content from a provider's location.

For symlinked content: removes the symlink.
For copied content: removes the copied file or directory.
For hooks/MCP: reverses the JSON merge from the provider's settings file.

The content remains in your library (~/.syllago/content/) and can be
reinstalled at any time with "syllago install".

flags.name: --dry-run
flags.description: Show what would happen without making changes

flags.name: --force
flags.description: Skip confirmation prompt

flags.name: --from
flags.description: Provider to uninstall from (omit to uninstall from all)

flags.name: --no-input
flags.description: Disable interactive prompts, use defaults

flags.name: --type
flags.description: Disambiguate when name exists in multiple types

commands.examples:
# Uninstall from a specific provider
  syllago uninstall my-skill --from claude-code

  # Uninstall from all providers
  syllago uninstall my-agent

  # Skip confirmation prompt
  syllago uninstall my-rule --from cursor --force

  # Preview what would happen
  syllago uninstall my-skill --dry-run

commands.name: update
commands.description: Check for and apply content updates from registries
commands.longDescription:
Compares installed content versions against their source registries
and updates items where a newer version is available.

Version comparison uses semver from .syllago.yaml metadata.
Items installed from local projects (not registries) are skipped.

flags.name: --all
flags.description: Update all installed items

flags.name: --dry-run
flags.description: Show what would be updated without changing anything

flags.name: --registry
flags.description: Only update items from this registry

commands.examples:
# Update a specific item
  syllago update my-skill

  # Check and update all installed items
  syllago update --all

  # Dry-run: show what would be updated
  syllago update --all --dry-run

  # Only update items from a specific registry
  syllago update --all --registry team-rules

commands.name: update
commands.description: Update syllago to the latest release
commands.examples: syllago update

commands.name: version
commands.description: Print syllago version
commands.examples: syllago version
